symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden watery non-bloody diarrhoea + vomiting + crampy pain + malaise

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden watery non-bloody diarrhoea with vomiting and malaise is typical of viral gastroenteritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No surgical-abdomen features (crampy, eases after stool, still passing diarrhoea)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Crampy pain easing after stool with ongoing diarrhoea argues strongly against appendicitis/obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blood/mucus, no high sustained fever/travel

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of blood/mucus/high fever/travel lowers the probability of invasive bacterial enterocolitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Clinical diagnosis; no routine stool tests for uncomplicated viral gastroenteritis.
If bloody diarrhoea, high fever, travel, immunosuppression, or prolonged/severe illness.
